Key Takeaways:
• Example 3: “Wildcraft” Quick Launch by John Gallagher
• Very short and simple launch - entire process was less than 1,000 words
• Timing
o 3 emails and a landing page
o Overall it’s incredibly quick and simple
• Offer
o Bonus/bundle on a board game that John has been selling for years
o Discount off the normal price
• Triggers
o Reciprocity
o Story
o Scarcity
o Event-based
o Community
o Likeability
o Anticipation
• Email 1
o Triggers: reciprocity, likeability, story

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
o Quick mention in normal newsletter
o In the P.S. - mentions Wildcraft product (board game) is almost back on sale
▪ 50% off product plus a bonus
• Email 2
o Triggers: story, scarcity, event-based, reason why, community (and a CRUSHING
offer)
o Starts with event-based angle, and product had been off the market
o Mentions nearly 50% off
o Mentions bonuses (previously sold webinar that they get for free)
o Ends with scarcity and close out date
o Tip: just talking about community creates the community
o Sends them to the secret page where they can make the purchase
o Delivers lots of scarcity but in a very understated way
o Sale is exclusive to newsletter subscribers
o Scarce information about scarcity is even more powerful
• Email 3
o Triggers: scarcity, likeability, conversation
o Subject line: Oops...forgot to say… (performed very well)
o “I got a bunch of emails…” - social proof
o Flips perspective - if you want the bonus and didn’t want the main product, it’s the
same thing

-- 3 of 9 --

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• Email 4
o Triggers: story, scarcity, reason why, event-based, community (and a CRUSHING
offer)
o Final email - starts with scarcity
o Digs into the reason why…
o Creates a “we’re all in it together” feel
o Reiterate offer
o Sends them to hidden page
o Ends with thanking the community
o In total this process of writing emails took about 1 hour
▪ Resulted in 800 sales and $16,000 in sales
• There’s 3 different style Quick Launches for you
• Each one very profitable and effective with a limited amount of work
Quick Launch Final Summary & Action Steps:
• There are three types of Quick Launches: Jeff’s Tax Sale, Go Romance, and Wildcraft
• Determine if a Quick Launch is right for you and your business (see Quick Launch, Part 1)
• Decide what type of Quick Launch you want to perform to get this extra cash infusion
• Plan your launch and design it around a story
• Don’t overdo these and make sure you keep the integrity and positioning of your brand
strong

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Next up we have the Wild Craft launch by John Gallagher, our guy who is famous
for the food stamps to six figures.
This one is really cool because it was very profitable, but it was incredibly quick
and incredibly simple and easy. It was just three emails and a landing page. As you’ll see,
the emails were short and simple.
There was a very good offer, it was basically a bonus/bundle. It was this board
game that John has been selling for years. He basically bundled that with another bonus
and he gave a discount off the normal price of the game. The game was almost half-off,
plus he bundled in an offer. He really did have the crushing offer.
He hit a bunch of triggers. Even though it was short and sweet, there was
probably 1,000 words total between the three emails, maybe less, but he still hit a bunch
of triggers. Let’s go take a look and see how he did this, how he hit reciprocity, story,
scarcity, event based, community, likability, and anticipation.
I just told you there were three emails – actually there were four total, but the
first one was just a really quick mention in his normal newsletter. What you’re looking at
here is his normal newsletter. Here he hits reciprocity, likability, and story.
Up top he has his normal type of content telling how people can make an herbal
popsicle. There’s no pitch at all in this email. Down here in the P.S. he said, “Wild Craft is
just about back on sale. We’re also giving away the Kids and Herbs course. Find out
about next week’s celebration.”
Wild Craft is the board game. Here he says it’s just about back on sale, basically
they ran out of stock, he was out of games, so they were getting more games made up, so
it had been off the market for awhile.
Then he has people click through to his newsletter. This is what the newsletter
looked like. Here just some content going for the likability, the relationship building, the
reciprocity, and down here he talks about the board game, Wild Craft. There’s a photo of
the board game and he just talks about it being out of stock. “As you read this, the new
batch of board games are just arriving. Next week we’ll be shipping with a three-day sale
that’s even better than the one we had for Christmas.” So they’re talking about 50% off,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 9 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
plus they’re giving a bonus of this Kids and Herbs class. This really is a crushing offer
here, 50% off plus a bonus.
Here he talks a little bit about the bonus. “We’ll email you next week about the
special sale just for our subscribers. The sale starts on June 22nd.” He’s starting to build
some anticipation in here as well.
Here’s the second email. This is actually the first full out email in the series. The
first one was just that little P.S. in John’s normal newsletter. This is a really cool email, it
has story, it has some great scarcity. John does it in a very understated way, but it’s full
out scarcity here. He makes it event based. He has the reason why, community, and the
crushing offer.
First of all, it’s the Wild Craft Summer Solstice – summer solstice is definitely
something that resonates with the people that John is publishing to, so right off the bat
it’s a little bit event based. It’s also event based because the product has been off the
market for awhile, because it was out of stock. Going with that summer solstice event, he
mentions that, he mentions the nearly 50% off.
I like that about this, because 50% off really is a fantastic deal on these Quick
Launches, it’s a magic number. When you go with 50% off, it’s just magic in terms of
conversion.
Here John is calling it “nearly 50%.” It’s really not 50%, but he’s doing “almost
50%.” He’s actually getting more than 50%.
“I know that might be enough for you, but we’re going to do something really
unusual here. We’re going to give you this seven-hour webinar course.” The beautiful
thing here is, as far as I know, it’s a downloadable course, so John’s cost of delivery is
zero. But he’s giving huge value.
He talks a little bit about the bonus here. Builds value. “We sold it for $37 and
today you get it for nothing.” Here’s a list of the bonuses. He really does create the
crushing offer, “You get all of this for $19.99. In five years online, I don’t think we’ve ever
given so much for so little.”
Now we bring in the scarcity. “This offer is gone at midnight on Thursday June
24th.” John does a really good job of writing a very conversational tone with no pressure
and no hype, but he still hits all the triggers.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 9 --

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Here’s building the community, “Thank you for being a subscriber of Herb
Mentor News. We appreciate you being a part of our community.” It’s funny how just
talking about community sort of makes a community. Someone might be reading this
who never thought of themselves as part of this community, but if you mention it
consistently, like John does, then you get a lot of the benefits out of the community
trigger.
Then he gave them the secret page where they can go buy it.
“In a week or so the game will be back on our site for $37 and Kids and Herbs will
be gone. If this sounds good to you, I’d go here now.” This is full out arm twisting
scarcity, but John does it in his understated style. It’s just a great use of scarcity, a great
match for the market.
Further down he talks about how people just absolutely loved the bonus offer, the
Kids and Herbs. This is really sweet, “Please keep this email if you do not order today, as
you cannot find this offer anywhere within Learning Herbs. This link is exclusive to
subscribers. This will disappear on Thursday at midnight.”
First of all, big time scarcity in this paragraph. Second of all, building the feeling
of exclusivity for subscribers. Building the feeling of community among subscribers.
Also, there’s a really cool thing here, having scarce information. Scarce information is
one of the strongest forms of scarcity. Here he is basically telling his subscribers that you
have this scarce information.
It’s not only this fantastic deal, but it’s this fantastic deal that only you know
about. Only the people are subscribed. Normal, regular people, outsiders don’t know
about it. Only the insiders subscribed to this newsletter get to know about this.
This is something to remember. Scarce information about scarcity is double
powerful. It’s like extra uber scarcity powerful.
Next email, this is the third email that I’m showing you, but it’s really only the
second in the sequence. First of all, “Ooops! Forgot to say…” is the subject line. John told
me that subject line converted really well, lots of opens and lots of click throughs.
“I just got a bunch of emails,” and this is basically talking about the bonus.
Talking about a bunch of emails builds the social proof.
He has basically found a way to talk about this bonus and bring it up. It’s not just
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 9 --

8
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
coming out and pitching you on it, it’s “I got a bunch of emails asking me about this,”
and then he talked about it.
“This is the official re-release. You couldn’t get this before, now you can get it.
The really cool part is that we’re giving it away. If you want it, go get it here.”
Here John apologizes for sending a couple of extra emails, because this is going
to a launch list, this is going to his main list.
Down here he talks about the flip between the bonus and the main offer. A lot of
times you can sell people on the bonus more than the main offer, in this type of case
where the board game and the bonus Kids and Herbs basically have the same retail
value.
Moving on to number four. I have some notes we’ll go over in a second, but John
hits story, scarcity, reason why, event based, community, and it’s a crushing offer.
Here the subject is, “Why we’re having the Wild Craft sale. Tonight June 24th at
midnight, it’s over.” He’s hitting scarcity right in the very first sentence. Great, succinct
and powerful.
“The 50% off will go away.” Again, very powerful scarcity right in the beginning.
“Why are we giving this away?” Now he’s into the reason why. “For the first time in three
years we were out of games and it took us four months to get them back in stock. It’s
amazing how challenging it was for a small home business to get a bank loan, but we did
it. We kind of feel like we owe you for being out of games for so long.” This is the “we’re
all in it together.” If you can get your clients and prospects, the people you’re writing to,
to feel like they’re in it with you, they’re in the thick of battle with you, then that’s a win.
Once again they reiterate the offer. “You get it all for $19.99.” It ends at midnight,
so big scarcity. Here’s your hidden page. John is great, almost every sentence in here is
hitting another trigger. The hidden page is that scarce information about scarcity, it’s
feeling like you’re an insider just because you’re reading this newsletter.
“Thanks again for being part of the community.” John again is going back to the
community. You build a community by just talking about the community.
That was it – basically those three emails, plus the initial announcement.
Just some notes from John up here…” That was it for the launch. Super easy to
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 9 --

9
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
write, about an hour of extra work.”
As you can see, there was basically just three extra emails and they were very
short, very succinct. Remember, the relationship with the list was already built since this
was going to people that I give great free stuff to.
“The launch never went to a sub-list. It’s rare I do sales like this to the entire list –
really rare – so I can get away with it every so often without annoying people.”
Normally he would lead people off onto a sub-list and then do a launch, but here
he just did three quick emails. As you noticed, he apologized a few places in those emails.
He just did three emails to his main list.
Note that there is a 50% off sale, plus a free webinar course. The offer is
irresistible and the offer is key. This is something I’m stressing over and over again in the
course, getting the offer right and make a crushing offer is key.
Three emails sent that week. These emails that he said he took about an hour,
plus a sale to a different list that week, resulted in 800 sales and approximately $16,000
in sales.
Three quick emails and about one hour’s worth of work, and he did $16,000 in
sales. That shows that a Quick Launch can be incredibly powerful.
That’s it. There’s three Quick Launches for you, each one of them fairly different.
The tax sale did not have a Prelaunch really; it went right to the offer. Go Romance had
more lengthy drawn out process. The Wild Craft was just very quick and dirty. All of
them were very profitable.
Quick Launches are really cool. If you can find a place and a way to drop them
into your business, it’s virtually instant money with almost no work whatsoever. There’s
some work, but without much work at all.
